hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if every `sysopt.xr-*` card can only promote against a `gap_source_handoff` packet that resolves inside the current autonomous truth surfaces, so route-neutral system work cannot inherit XR evidence that exists only in an external runtime mirror.
measurement: Before the candidate ratchet, the XR control-plane contract only pinned `gap_source_handoff` as a string. Baseline falsification showed `test -f 'CLAW/control-plane/runtime/handoffs/C4-20260404T131253Z-04.json'` returned missing in this worktree and `test -f '/Users/zer0palab/Zer0pa Website/Website-main/CLAW/control-plane/runtime/truth-cache/packets/C4-20260403T165341Z-03.json'` also returned missing, while `test -f '/Users/zer0palab/Zer0pa Website/Website-main/CLAW/control-plane/runtime/handoffs/C4-20260404T131253Z-04.json'` still returned present in the external Website-main runtime mirror. Tightening `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` to require local or shared-cache handoff resolution would therefore turn the current XR ratchet inventory red before the bounded evaluation bundle even ran. The candidate was rejected instead of promoted.
keep_rule: Keep only if the authoritative `/work/xr` gap `source_handoff` resolves inside the current worktree runtime or the declared shared truth cache, and the tightened verifier plus fixed scope-local evaluation bundle pass without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: XR handoff-resolution locks cannot promote until the authoritative `/work/xr` rejection packet is materialized inside the current worktree runtime or shared truth cache; today it only resolves through the external Website-main runtime mirror.
rejection_reason: The candidate depends on XR handoff packets that are missing from both this worktree runtime surface and the declared shared truth cache; only the external Website-main runtime mirror still contains the packet.
